commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the unknown-model hint for `agents.defaults.models` entries with `agentRuntime.id` and adds a focused regression test.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +23. Total +33 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source reproduction: current main emits the provider-registration hint when an allowlisted runtime-backed model lacks a provider row, and the docs/provider code show Codex-backed models come from runtime/catalog discovery. I did not run the live ChatGPT/Codex account path in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(models): don't advise models.providers[] registration for runtime-bound models
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users whose agent default model is served by an agent
runtime (most commonly the `codex` runtime backed by an OpenAI ChatGPT
account) would have **every message fail** once the configured model id is
retired by the provider — with a misleading recovery hint that sends them
down the wrong path.

Concretely: a config pinned to `openai/gpt-5.3-codex` via
`agents.defaults.models["openai/gpt-5.3-codex"].agentRuntime.id = "codex"`
started failing on every turn after OpenAI stopped offering `gpt-5.3-codex`
to ChatGPT-account Codex. Model resolution surfaced:

```
Unknown model: openai/gpt-5.3-codex. Found agents.defaults.models["openai/gpt-5.3-codex"],
but no matching models.providers["openai"].models[] entry. Add { "id": "gpt-5.3-codex", ... }
to models.providers["openai"].models[] to register this provider model.
```

Following that hint (adding the `models.providers[]` entry) makes resolution
*succeed* — only for the request to then be rejected by the API:

```
400 invalid_request_error: The 'gpt-5.3-codex' model is not supported when
using Codex with a ChatGPT account.
```

So the guidance actively leads users away from the real fix (switching to a
model the runtime still offers, e.g. `gpt-5.5`).

## Why This Change Was Made

Models bound to an agent runtime draw their catalog from that runtime and its
linked account, **not** from `models.providers[].models[]`. So when
`buildMissingProviderModelRegistrationHint` sees that the configured
`agents.defaults.models` entry carries an `agentRuntime`, it no longer advises
a `models.providers[]` registration. Instead it explains that the model comes
from the runtime/account and points the user at the runtime's live catalog
(`openclaw models list --provider <id>`) and at switching
`agents.defaults.model.primary` to a currently available model. Behavior for
non-runtime (plain provider) models is unchanged.

## User Impact

Users hitting a retired runtime-backed model now get an actionable error that
leads to the correct fix on the first try, instead of a registration hint that
appears to work but produces a downstream provider `400`.

## Evidence

- New focused unit test in `model.test.ts` asserting the exact error for a
  `codex`-runtime-bound entry; the existing plain-provider registration-hint
  test is unchanged (the new branch only triggers when `agentRuntime` is set).
- Reproduced the original failure and confirmed the real cause live against an
  affected install (codex app-server offered `gpt-5.5`/`gpt-5.4`/`gpt-5.4-mini`
  but not `gpt-5.3-codex`); switching the default to `openai/gpt-5.5` restored
  normal operation.

> AI-assisted: investigated and authored with Claude Code.
target.author: romneyda
